subject: Re: Export star file of tilt series ctf
body:
Brilliant!
Many thanks!

On Fri, 20 May 2022 at 04:49 Huy Bui <huybk81@gmail.com> wrote:

> Ok, it is working. Here's the code:
>
> #!/usr/bin/env python3
> # -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
>
> import numpy as np
> import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
> import glob
>
> if __name__=='__main__':
>     xmlfiles = glob.glob('*.mrc.xml')
>
>     for xmlfile in xmlfiles:
>         ts = ET.parse(xmlfile)
>         outfile = xmlfile.replace('mrc.xml', '_output.txt')
>         root = ts.getroot()
>
>         z = []
>         data = []
>         ctfResEst = float(root.attrib["CTFResolutionEstimate"])
>
>         gridctf = root.find('GridCTF')
>         for node in gridctf.iter('Node'):
>             z.append(node.attrib['Z'])
>
>         for zvalue in z:
>             nodectf = root.find(f"./GridCTF/Node/[@Z='{zvalue}']")
>             nodectfdelta =
> root.find(f"./GridCTFDefocusDelta/Node/[@Z='{zvalue}']")
>             nodeangle =
> root.find(f"./GridCTFDefocusAngle/Node/[@Z='{zvalue}']")
>             defocusv = float(nodectf.attrib['Value'])*10000
>             defocusu = float(nodectfdelta.attrib['Value'])*10000 + defocusv
>             angle = float(nodeangle.attrib['Value'])
>             data.append([float(zvalue) + 1, defocusu, defocusv, angle, 0,
> 0, ctfResEst])
>
>         np.savetxt(outfile, data, fmt='%.2f', delimiter=' ')
>
>
> On Thursday, May 19, 2022 at 7:39:28 PM UTC-4 Huy Bui wrote:
>
>> Now, looking at the XML file.
>>
>>     <GridCTF Width="1" Height="1" Depth="39" MarginX="0" MarginY="0"
>> MarginZ="0">
>>         <Node X="0" Y="0" Z="0" Value="4.663075" />
>> ...
>>     </GridCTF>
>>     <GridCTFDefocusDelta Width="1" Height="1" Depth="39" MarginX="0"
>> MarginY="0" MarginZ="0">
>>         <Node X="0" Y="0" Z="0" Value="0.5025561" />
>> ....
>>     </GridCTFDefocusDelta>
>>     <GridCTFDefocusAngle Width="1" Height="1" Depth="39" MarginX="0"
>> MarginY="0" MarginZ="0">
>>         <Node X="0" Y="0" Z="0" Value="138" />
>> ...
>>     </GridCTFDefocusAngle>
>>
>> Is the value in GridCTF Value 4.663075 equivalent to DefocusU to CTFFind?
>> (Or DefocusV???)
>>
>> Then DefocusV = GridCTF_Value + GridCTFDefocusDelta_Value?
>>
>> Best,
>> H
>> On Thursday, May 19, 2022 at 7:30:56 PM UTC-4 Huy Bui wrote:
>>
>>> Thanks. I was hoping I can save time writing a python script :) But your
>>> template is already helping me.
>>> Anyway, I can work on that tomorrow and send back the script here if I
>>> got it right :)
>>>
>>> Best,
>>> H
>>>
>>>
>>> On Thursday, May 19, 2022 at 7:21:39 PM UTC-4 Pranav Shah wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Huy,
>>>> You will have to mine the XML file associated with each tilt series to
>>>> get the information you need. In the past I have used the lxml library
>>>> to scrape this information. Below is a template that you could develop
>>>> on.
>>>>
>>>> /////
>>>>
>>>> from lxml import etree
>>>> xmlfiles = glob.glob("Position_03.xml")
>>>>
>>>> tilt_names = []
>>>> for index, xmlfile in enumerate(xmlfiles):
>>>> tree = etree.parse(xmlfile)
>>>>
>>>> #Uncomment these lines to browse all available paths in the xml
>>>> '''
>>>> all_elements = list(tree.iter())
>>>> print([element.tag for element in all_elements])
>>>> '''
>>>> tilt_name_nodes = tree.findall(".//MoviePath")
>>>> for ts_name in tilt_name_nodes:
>>>> text = ts_name.text
>>>> names = text.split('\n')
>>>> tilt_names.append(names)
>>>> ///
>>>> Hope there's enough in there to get you started and improve on!
>>>> :)
>>>> Best,
>>>> Pranav
>>>> --
>>>> Pranav Shah
>>>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>>>
>>>> Division of Structural Biology,
>>>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>>>> University of Oxford,
>>>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>>>> UK
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Fri, May 20, 2022 at 12:01 AM Huy Bui <huy...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>> >
>>>> > Hi all,
>>>> >
>>>> > I just started using Warp and I have to say it is great.
>>>> > I don't use Warp in the relion 3.1 subtomo workflow but for relion
>>>> 4.0
>>>> > I did 2D processing, tilt series alignment using autoalign, then back
>>>> to Warp for tilt series CTF estimation.
>>>> > I found the ctf from tilt series CTF estimation from Warp look great
>>>> with the constraint estimation vs the one that I got from Ctffind4 due to
>>>> the low signal from high tilt in my tilt series.
>>>> > Is there any way to export the CTF estimation from Warp into a star
>>>> file with CTF info from each view of the tomogram? I can convert it to
>>>> ctffind4 format to import into Relion 4 later.
>>>> >
>>>> > Best,
>>>> > Huy
>>>> >
